symptoms.symptom_name: Painless full-thickness plantar ulcer with peripheral callus in a neuropathic foot

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A painless callus-rimmed plantar ulcer in a neuropathic diabetic is the classic neuropathic ulcer.
Arterial ulcers are painful with cold, pulseless feet.

symptoms.symptom_name: Malodorous seropurulent discharge with perilesional erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Signs of local infection in a deep ulcer indicate a limb-threatening infection needing urgent care.
Deep infected ulcers over bone raise osteomyelitis risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bounding pedal pulses, warm foot; total loss of 10g monofilament sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Warm, well-perfused, insensate foot confirms neuropathic (not ischaemic) aetiology.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Foot X-ray (± MRI) for osteomyelitis and probe-to-bone at in-person review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Deep chronic infected ulcer requires evaluation for bone involvement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab/deep tissue culture and bloods (FBC, CRP, glucose/HbA1c)

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baseline perfusion for healing, even with palpable pulses.
